businessSummary.oneLiner: Southeast Asia's largest e-commerce (Shopee), digital entertainment (Garena/Free Fire), and fintech (Monee) operator — Singapore-based global internet company
businessSummary.description: Sea Limited is a Singapore-based global technology company. Q4 2025 revenue $6.85B (+38.4% YoY), net income $397M, EPS $0.77. FY2025 revenue $22.94B (+36.4%). Closed at $91.98 (-0.35%) on 3/6. Minor pullback after prior day's +5.1% rebound. Gradually recovering from the -26% post-earnings crash on 3/3. Held up well despite the jobs shock + oil surge macro selloff. Forward P/E ~21.5x, PEG 0.35.
howTheyMakeMoney: E-commerce (Shopee): #1 e-commerce platform in SEA. GMV-based commissions and advertising. Q4 revenue +35.8%
Digital Financial Services (Monee/SeaMoney): Digital payments, lending, insurance. Q4 revenue +54.3%, loan book $9.2B (+80%)
Digital Entertainment (Garena): Free Fire and other game operations. Q4 bookings +23.8% YoY
keyProducts: Shopee: #1 e-commerce platform in Southeast Asia and Latin America
Free Fire: Globally popular battle royale mobile game
SeaMoney/Monee: Digital payments and financial services, $9.2B loan book

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Attractive when factoring in 2026 earnings growth

keyMetrics.name: PEG Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: Well below 1 — significantly undervalued relative to growth

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Improving profitability, but VIP program investments pressuring margins

keyMetrics.name: Cash Holdings
keyMetrics.interpretation: Massive cash position. Extremely ample reinvestment capacity

growthDrivers.title: Held Up in Macro Selloff — Only -0.35% Decline
growthDrivers.description: While Nasdaq plunged on jobs shock + oil surge, SE limited its decline to -0.35%. Three consecutive days of stability after the 3/3 -26% crash.

growthDrivers.title: Seeking Alpha: 'Panic Created a Rare Strong Buy Opportunity'
growthDrivers.description: Post-earnings panic selling created oversold conditions. With $8.73B net cash and strong fundamentals, this is a buying opportunity.

growthDrivers.title: Monee Fintech Acceleration — Loan Book $9.2B (+80%)
growthDrivers.description: AI-powered underwriting expanding lending access. 37M active credit users, NPL 1.1% healthy. Fintech growth engine.

growthDrivers.title: Google AI Partnership — AI Tools Across Shopee, Monee, Garena
growthDrivers.description: PYMNTS: Sea partnered with Google to deploy AI-powered innovations across all core business lines.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 52% SEA e-commerce market share (Shopee), fintech-ecommerce-gaming ecosystem synergy, and $10.57B net cash are the core moats.

moats.description: Shopee holds 52% market share in Southeast Asia. Vertically integrated logistics (Shopee Express).
Monee (SeaMoney) provides payments and lending to Shopee users. Maximum cross-selling.
Globally popular game generating stable cash flows to fund e-commerce investment.

competitors.name: TikTok Shop
competitors.detail: Social commerce growing rapidly in SEA. Key threat

competitors.name: MercadoLibre
competitors.detail: Latin American e-commerce. Competing with Shopee's LatAm operations

recentNews.headline: SE -0.35% amid macro selloff, stable consolidation
recentNews.significance: Held up well despite jobs shock + oil surge. Bottom formation signal after 3/3 crash

recentNews.headline: SE +5.1% rebound as investors reassess post-earnings selloff
recentNews.significance: Investors refocusing on Q4 revenue beat and strong growth trajectory

recentNews.headline: Simply Wall St: Revenue beat but margin concerns keep 30-day return at -22.3%
recentNews.significance: EPS miss and cautious margin guidance remain the root cause of the selloff

recentNews.headline: Seeking Alpha: 'Market panic created a rare Strong Buy opportunity'
recentNews.significance: Net cash $8.73B, revenue +38% company entering oversold territory

recentNews.headline: Google AI partnership — AI tools for Shopee, Monee, Garena
recentNews.significance: AI deployment across all business segments for operational efficiency

analystOpinions.notableComment: 21 analysts, Strong Buy consensus. Target $178.45 implies +94% upside — record gap to current price. PEG 0.35 signals extreme undervaluation relative to growth.

risks.title: EPS miss + cautious margin guidance
risks.description: Q4 EPS $0.63 missed consensus by $0.28. VIP program investments and competition pressuring margins.

risks.title: TikTok Shop competition intensifying
risks.description: Social commerce growing rapidly in SEA, threatening Shopee's market share.

risks.title: Macro headwinds — oil $90+, emerging market risk
risks.description: Oil price surge could dampen SEA consumer sentiment. Inflation re-acceleration concern.

risks.title: Insider selling
risks.description: 3/2 filings: Perennial Spring $9.6M, Green Kiwi $22.5M planned sales.

risks.title: Monee lending risk
risks.description: Loan book $9.2B (+80%). NPL could rise in economic downturn.

buyReasons.title: PEG 0.35 — extreme undervaluation vs growth
buyReasons.rationale: 38% revenue growth with PEG at 0.35. Most attractive valuation among growth stocks.

buyReasons.title: 94% discount to analyst consensus $178
buyReasons.rationale: 21 Strong Buy analysts. Record gap to consensus. Oversold territory.

buyReasons.title: Net cash $10.57B — fortress balance sheet
buyReasons.rationale: 19% of market cap in cash. Downside risk limited, abundant reinvestment capacity.

buyReasons.title: Monee fintech +54% growth + Google AI partnership
buyReasons.rationale: Loan book $9.2B (+80%), NPL 1.1%. AI tools boosting operational efficiency.

overallOpinion: On a Friday when the entire market tumbled due to a jobs shock and surging oil prices, SE barely moved, closing at $91.98 with just a -0.35% decline. After the -26% crash following its March 3 earnings report, the stock has stabilized for three straight days and appears to be forming a floor.

The PEG ratio sits at 0.35, meaning the stock is very cheap relative to its growth rate of 38% annual revenue gains. All 21 analysts covering the stock rate it a Strong Buy, with an average price target of $178, which is 94% above today's price.

The company holds $10.57 billion in net cash (cash minus debt), giving it a rock-solid balance sheet. Its fintech arm Monee grew revenue 54% year over year. The concern is that earnings per share fell short of expectations, and management gave a cautious outlook on profit margins, which has dampened investor confidence in the near term.

Heavy selling has pushed the stock into territory where a bounce often follows, and it is sitting just 12% above its one-year low, near rock bottom. The panic-driven selloff could turn into a buying opportunity for patient, long-term investors.
